nodes.label: Unit 6: Hiawatha / The Scholar's Mother Tongue
nodes.description: A poem about a boy who loved nature and a story about Birbal's cleverness

nodes.label: Hiawatha (Poem)
nodes.description: A poem about a young Native American boy who lived in a forest and learned from nature

nodes.label: Raised by Nokomis
nodes.description: Hiawatha was brought up by his old grandmother Nokomis in the forest

nodes.label: Learned About Birds
nodes.description: He learned names of all birds, their language, their nests, and their secrets

nodes.label: Learned About Animals
nodes.description: He learned the names of all the beasts and where they hid and lived

nodes.label: Love for Nature
nodes.description: The poem shows harmony between humans and the natural world

nodes.label: The Scholar's Mother Tongue (Story)
nodes.description: A story from Akbar's court about finding a scholar's mother tongue

nodes.label: Akbar's Challenge
nodes.description: Akbar asked courtiers to find the mother tongue of a pandit who spoke many languages

nodes.label: Birbal's Clever Plan
nodes.description: Birbal went to the scholar at night and tickled his ear while he slept

nodes.label: Startled Response
nodes.description: When surprised from sleep, the scholar cried out in his mother tongue

nodes.label: Grammar: Pronouns
nodes.description: Words that replace nouns to avoid repetition

nodes.label: Subject Pronouns
nodes.description: I, you, he, she, it, we, they - these do the action in a sentence

nodes.label: Object Pronouns
nodes.description: me, you, him, her, it, us, them - these receive the action

nodes.label: Remember!
nodes.description: A pronoun must match the noun it replaces in number and gender
